title: Arrival Burn
description: The destination system resolves from a bright dot into a place. Final approach requires a heavy deceleration burn.

outcomes.label: Textbook burn
outcomes.description: By the book, on the fuel budget.
outcomes.log: The arrival burn went by the book. The observation decks were crowded for a week.

title: The Halfway Beacon
description: The ship passes the midpoint marker its founders laid in the plan. Half the voyage is behind; no one living chose the far half.

outcomes.label: Mark the crossing
outcomes.description: A day of ceremony and rest. Morale answers.
outcomes.log: The ship marked the halfway crossing. For a day, the corridors felt like a procession — and the founders had left more than a marker: a ramscoop module, cached at the beacon, now folded into the salvage hold.

title: The Fork in the Dark
description: Two viable courses to the destination diverge here: a fast lane through a debris shelf, or a slow safe arc. The nav computer wants a decision it cannot make.

outcomes.label: Take the fast lane
outcomes.description: Shave years off the crossing. Bet the hull on good sensors.
outcomes.log: The ship threaded the debris shelf and gained a decade. The hull wears the toll.

outcomes.label: Take the safe arc
outcomes.description: Add years, keep the plating.
outcomes.log: The ship took the long safe arc. The schedule slipped; nothing else did.

title: First Light
description: For the first time, the destination star can be picked out with the naked eye from the observation deck — a specific point of light, not a coordinate.

outcomes.label: Gather and look
outcomes.description: Call the ship to the windows.
outcomes.log: The ship crowded the observation decks to see its destination as a star. Some had waited their whole lives to look.

title: The Final Approach
description: The founders wrote a final-approach plan for a ship they never saw finished. The council can ratify it as written or amend it with everything the voyage has learned.

outcomes.label: Ratify as written
outcomes.description: Trust the plan the founders sealed.
outcomes.log: The founders' approach plan was ratified unchanged. The ship will arrive exactly as it was told to.

outcomes.label: Amend with what we know
outcomes.description: The voyage taught things the founders could not know.
outcomes.log: The approach plan was amended with a lifetime of hard-won data. The founders would not recognize it; it is better.

title: The Scout Returns
description: A probe the founders flung ahead a lifetime ago has finally answered — a thin stream of data about the place the ship is aimed at. Some of it does not match the charts the voyage was planned around.

outcomes.label: Trust the new data
outcomes.description: Revise the plan around what the probe actually found.
outcomes.log: The probe's data was trusted over the founders' charts, and the approach was quietly rebuilt around a world no one had promised.

outcomes.label: Keep the founders' charts
outcomes.description: One probe is one probe. Trust the plan that got you here.
outcomes.log: The council kept faith with the founders' charts. The probe's warnings were filed, and the ship held the course it was given.

title: The Lodestar
description: The scouts find it on the outbound leg: a derelict archive-ship, holds heavy with charts and cold-stored fortune, drifting exactly where no chart said to look.

outcomes.label: Log it and press on
outcomes.description: The charter comes first; mark it for the ships that follow.
outcomes.log: The lodestar was logged, sampled, and left for the ships that would come after. The charter still called, and the ship answered.

outcomes.label: Claim it and turn home
outcomes.description: A find this rich is a mission of its own.
outcomes.log: The lodestar's holds gave up a fortune in charts and metal. With wealth like this aboard, the council saw no reason to sail farther — the ship came about for home, heavy and triumphant.

title: The Wayfarers
description: Another ship hails across the dark — a generation vessel like our own, centuries into its own long errand, its people as strange and as familiar as a reflection. They propose an exchange of charts, craft, and stories.

outcomes.label: Open the exchange
outcomes.description: Trade knowledge freely with a fellow wanderer.
outcomes.log: For a month the two ships flew as one, trading charts and songs and the hard-won tricks of the long dark. Both sailed on richer, and a little changed by the meeting.

outcomes.label: Keep our distance
outcomes.description: Exchange pleasantries and nothing more. Trust is a luxury.
outcomes.log: The two ships exchanged careful greetings and careful silences, and parted with charts unshared. The elders called it prudence; the young called it a door left shut.

title: The Wary Frontier
description: The ship enters the claimed space of a civilization that has clearly been burned before — their hails are demands, their escorts are armed, their welcome is a warning. They want to know exactly what this vessel is and why it is here.

outcomes.label: Answer with full honesty
outcomes.description: Open the logs. Let them see the mission entire.
outcomes.log: The council opened the ship's records to the wary locals, mission and all. The escorts stood down by degrees, and safe passage was granted to a vessel that had nothing to hide.

outcomes.label: Give guarded answers
outcomes.description: Reveal only what safety requires. Keep the rest close.
outcomes.log: The ship gave the frontier only what it had to and slipped through under a cloud of suspicion. The locals logged this hull as one that keeps secrets, and passed the word ahead.

outcomes.label: Detour around their reach
outcomes.description: Answer nothing. A people burned before will not be reassured by words; give them the one thing they actually want, which is your absence. Burn wide around their claimed space and leave them their peace.
outcomes.log: The voyage gave the wary frontier the only gift it trusted — distance. The ship burned wide around their reach, spending fuel it would rather have kept, and left no wake in their space and no story in their logs. Some aboard chafed at the caution; more slept easier knowing no gun had tracked them that night.

title: The Young World
description: The route passes a world of people who have not yet left their atmosphere — pre-industrial, unaware, gazing up at a new star that is really our drive-flare. To reveal ourselves would change their history forever; to hide is its own kind of choice.

outcomes.label: Reveal ourselves
outcomes.description: Descend, and become gods or teachers in their sky.
outcomes.log: The ship revealed itself to the young world and watched its own arrival become the founding myth of another people. Whether gift or wound, it could never be taken back.

outcomes.label: Pass unseen
outcomes.description: Douse the flare, run dark, leave their sky unchanged.
outcomes.log: The ship ran dark past the young world and let it keep its innocent sky. Some aboard grieved the hands they did not extend; the council called restraint its own kind of gift.

complications.description_add: And there is a grief in it this generation that the founders never could have felt: the crew who look down on that living world are, in the flesh, no longer able to live on one. Here is the very thing the whole voyage was launched to find — an open sky, a real gravity, ground — and the shipborn people gazing at it have become a kind that could not stand beneath that sky and would not survive that ground. They have found the shore and lost the legs to walk it.
complications.log: The young world passed beneath a crew who could no longer have lived on it — the founders' dream of open ground arriving a generation too late for the bodies that reached it — and the ship carried its quiet, particular grief on into a dark that had, at last, become the only home its people could keep.

title: The Silent Monuments
description: Drifting in the deep are the works of a people long gone — vast, patient structures of unknown purpose, older than the ship's whole line of ancestors. They could hold knowledge, danger, or only echoes.

outcomes.label: Send teams into the ruins
outcomes.description: Spend the time and the risk to learn what the builders left.
outcomes.log: The teams went into the silent monuments and came back awed, laden, and fewer. What they learned reshaped the ship's understanding of the dark; what took the lost, they never named.

outcomes.label: Record them and move on
outcomes.description: Scan from range. Some tombs are best left sealed.
outcomes.log: The ship mapped the monuments from a respectful distance and left them to their long silence. The archives gained a mystery and the crew kept its full number.

complications.description_add: And this is a ship whose whole name is the chasing of exactly this — a chronicle thick with charted impossibilities, a crew who have made a creed of wonder — so the debate is not really whether to enter the monuments but how deep, and how long, and how much the ship will spend to read what the vanished builders left. A vessel famous for its marvels does not sail past the largest marvel it has ever found.
complications.log: A ship that had made wonder its name went into the silent monuments the way it went into everything strange — all the way, and gladly — and gathered from the vanished builders' works a haul of knowledge worthy of its reputation, at a cost in the lost it accepted as the price of being the kind of ship it had chosen to be.

title: The Last Broadcast
description: A beacon drifts on the lane, still faithfully repeating a message in no known tongue after who knows how many centuries alone. Its makers are surely dust. The signal waits for an answer it was built to expect.

outcomes.label: Answer the beacon
outcomes.description: Send the reply it has waited eons for, wherever it goes.
outcomes.log: The ship broadcast an answer into the dark on the beacon's own frequency, completing a handshake begun before the founders were born. Nothing replied. It felt, somehow, like a kindness owed.

outcomes.label: Take it and go silent
outcomes.description: Recover the beacon's tech; leave no trace of ourselves.
outcomes.log: The salvage crews took the beacon apart for its patient, ancient tech and left the lane silent behind them. The message it had carried for eons ended, unanswered, in a cargo hold.

complications.description_add: And to a crew that has itself drifted far from the ones who launched it, the beacon is not a curiosity but a mirror: here is a people who outlived their purpose, who kept faithfully repeating a message in a tongue their own descendants — if any survived — could no longer have read, broadcasting into a dark that had long forgotten them. The ship's elders look at the endless, unanswered signal and see, uncomfortably clearly, a thing the ship itself could one day become.
complications.log: The drifted ship heard its own possible epitaph in the beacon's patient, pointless faithfulness — a people reduced to a message no one was left to understand — and whatever it chose to do with the thing, it carried on a little more soberly, having met a stranger's ending that looked too much like a road its own long drift might yet be traveling.

title: The Long Watched
description: The signals analysts find it first, and then everyone knows: the ship has been watched. Not now — for years. Something out in the dark has been quietly observing the vessel's passage, tracking it, listening, and never once announcing itself. There is no threat in it that anyone can point to, and no reassurance either; only the deeply unsettling knowledge that the ship has not been alone in a long time, and that whatever shares the dark with it has chosen, so far, to say nothing. The council must decide who speaks first.

outcomes.label: Hail them first
outcomes.description: Break the silence the ship did not know it was keeping. Broadcast a greeting, plainly and openly, and announce to whatever has been watching that the ship knows it is there and would rather meet than be studied.
outcomes.log: The ship hailed the dark that had been watching it, choosing to be a greeter rather than a specimen — and waited, every antenna strained, for whatever would choose to answer a hand extended into a silence that had studied it for years.

outcomes.label: Slip away quiet
outcomes.description: Whatever is out there has had every chance to speak and has not. Change nothing, reveal nothing, and carry the ship on as if it had never noticed — and let the watchers keep their silence, and the crew their unease.
outcomes.log: The ship pretended it had not noticed and flew on unchanged, ceding the first word to the watchers who had never offered it — and carried, from then on, the quiet weight of knowing it was seen, and had chosen not to look back.

complications.description_add: And on a ship drifted this far from the founders' cool rationalism, the analysts' talk of an unknown observer falls on a crew that has already decided what the watchers are: not aliens but a presence — ancestors keeping vigil, gods weighing the voyage, the founders themselves come back to see how their children fared. The council is no longer debating a first-contact protocol but a matter of faith, and the two are not easy to keep apart.
complications.log: The ship's drifted generations did not hear *aliens* in the analysts' report; they heard *watchers* in the older sense — the vigilant dead, the founders' ghosts, something owed reverence — and the council's careful protocol dissolved into a quarrel over what the ship owed a presence half of it had already begun to pray to.

title: The Offered Road
description: A civilization the ship never expected — old, settled, and casually more advanced than anything the founders imagined — makes contact, and makes an offer. They will open a faster road: a corridor through folded space that would cut generations off the crossing, deliver the ship to its destination in a fraction of the dark still ahead. The price is not blood or treasure. It is the ship's archive — the full record of the founding, the voyage, the people it has been — copied into their keeping, to be studied and kept and, they say gently, remembered long after the ship itself is dust. A shortcut home, for the ship's whole story told to strangers.

outcomes.label: Keep the long road
outcomes.description: Some things are not for copying, even to save generations. Thank the strangers, decline the corridor, and keep the ship's story the ship's own — and fly the whole long dark the founders always meant it to.
outcomes.log: The ship kept its own story and its own long road, declining a shortcut bought with the whole record of who it had been — a costly pride, generations added back to the crossing, and a people who would arrive as themselves or not at all.

outcomes.label: Trade the archive for the road
outcomes.description: A story kept and a shortcut taken are worth more than a story hoarded and a voyage that outlasts everyone aboard. Give the strangers the archive, take the folded road, and let the ship's whole history live on in alien keeping while the ship itself, for once, arrives early.
outcomes.log: The ship gave its whole story into alien hands and took the folded road home, trading the privacy of its history for generations of the dark it would no longer have to cross — its founding now a record in a stranger's library, and its arrival, at last, within a living crew's reach.

outcomes.label: Bargain as their kindred
outcomes.description: The Ascension Circle recognize these strangers as something like what the ship is becoming, and can treat with them accordingly — not as a primitive accepting a gift, but as a lesser kin negotiating terms. Let the augmented deal, and win the road *and* the archive both, at a price paid only in how much further from the founders the bargain leaves the crew.
outcomes.log: Under the Ascension Circle the ship met the strangers not as a supplicant but as a lesser kind of kin, and bargained accordingly — winning the folded road while keeping its own archive, and paying only in the further distance the negotiation put between the crew and the baseline humanity the founders had launched.
